stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( [[a]]y + [[b]],\; [[a]]x + [[c]] \right) \) is conservative on all of \( \mathbb{R}^{2} \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]]) \).
solution: A potential is \( \varphi(x,y) = [[a]]xy + [[b]]x + [[c]]y \), since \( \nabla\varphi = \mathbf{F} \). By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This keeps only the \( xy \)-part of the potential, dropping the linear terms.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( [[a]]y + [[b]]z,\; [[a]]x + [[c]]z,\; [[b]]x + [[c]]y \right) \) is conservative on all of \( \mathbb{R}^{3} \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]], [[z1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]], [[z2]]) \).
solution: A potential is \( \varphi(x,y,z) = [[a]]xy + [[b]]xz + [[c]]yz \), since \( \nabla\varphi = \mathbf{F} \). By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This keeps only the \( xy \)-term of the potential, dropping the other two cross terms.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( [[a]]y^{2},\; 2\cdot[[a]]xy + [[b]] \right) \) is conservative on all of \( \mathbb{R}^{2} \), with potential \( \varphi(x,y) = [[a]]xy^{2} + [[b]]y \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]]) \).
solution: By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, since \( \mathbf{F}=\nabla\varphi \), \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This drops the linear \( [[b]]y \) term of the potential at both endpoints.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( [[a]]x + [[b]]y,\; kx + [[c]]y \right) \) is conservative for exactly one value of the constant \( k \). Find that value of \( k \).
solution: For \( \mathbf{F}=(P,Q) \) to be conservative on \( \mathbb{R}^{2} \), it is necessary and sufficient (since the domain is simply connected) that \( \partial_y P = \partial_x Q \), i.e. \( [[b]] = k \). So \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This confuses the required value with the unrelated coefficient of \( x \) in \( P \).
This confuses the required value with the unrelated coefficient of \( y \) in \( Q \).
This has the wrong sign; the test requires \( \partial_y P = \partial_x Q \), i.e. \( k=b \), not \( k=-b \).
This adds two unrelated coefficients instead of matching the mixed partial derivatives.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
